personality_one_liner: A highly trustworthy, data-dense, and professional command center for real estate investors, relying on clean structure, tabular typography, and a grounded earth-toned palette rather than flashy AI tropes.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical breathing room for standard UI, but tighter density for financial/scoring data blocks to ensure scanability.

dont's: Applying to data-dense sections where clarity is paramount

screens.name: Listings Feed (Home)
screens.purpose: High-density feed of investment properties with robust filtering.

states.loading: Skeleton cards mimicking the layout of property images and data rows.
states.empty: Illustration of a house with a magnifying glass + 'No properties match your exact filters. Try broadening your criteria.'
states.error: 'Unable to load Tarrant County listings' + Retry Button.

screens.layout: Sticky top header containing a search bar focused on Tarrant County. Immediately below the header, a sticky horizontal ScrollView (shows scroll indicator false) acting as a chip row for the 12+ investor filters (REO, Cash Buyer, High Equity, etc.). The main content is a FlatList of property cards. Each card has a 140pt tall top image with an expo-linear-gradient scrim at the bottom containing the Price and Est. ROI. Below the image, the card body displays the Address, an Owner Intelligence badge (brandTertiary background), and a compact 3-column footer for Investment Score, Equity, and Risk.

screens.name: Property Profile
screens.purpose: Deep dive into financial viability, ownership intelligence, and AI deal scoring.

states.empty: N/A - Data should be present if navigated here.
states.error: Error loading property details + Retry Button.

screens.layout: Full-bleed scrolling screen. Hero is a large property photo (height 280pt) with a top gradient scrim for a glass back button. A sticky primary CTA ('Save Deal' / 'Contact Owner') lives at the bottom of the screen. Section 1 (Owner Intelligence): Clean card showing Owner Name, Type (Badge), Mailing vs Situs Address. Section 2 (AI Deal Scoring): A 2-column grid displaying the 5 scoring metrics (Investment, Wholesale, Flip, Rental, Risk) using clear progress bars or circular rings with tabular numbers. Section 3 (Financials): Key-value rows with hairline dividers for Values, Taxes, Equity Est, Est ROI. Section 4: A small map snippet showing nearby foreclosures/investor purchases.

property_suburb.alt: Suburban house in Texas with well-maintained lawn at dusk

property_modest.alt: Modest single family home with a car parked in front

property_fix_flip.alt: Workers replacing the roof on a brick house, fix and flip style

instructions_to_main_agent: ZERO TOLERANCE: No blue, purple, or indigo colors anywhere. Use the specified dark moss and terracotta earth-toned palette.
Implement a sticky horizontal ScrollView for the 12+ investor filters (High Equity, Cash Buyer, etc.) directly below the search bar on the Home screen.
Use 'fontVariant: ["tabular-nums"]' for all pricing, scores, equity estimates, and ROI values to ensure digits align perfectly and convey a professional financial aesthetic.
Owner classification (LLC, Trust, Bank-Owned, Individual) MUST always be displayed as a distinct badge using the 'brandTertiary' background color, enabling quick scanning for investors.

In the Property Detail Profile, the 'AI Deal Scoring' section must feel like a data-rich command center. Use structured grids with progress bars or circular rings for the 5 metrics (Investment, Wholesale, Flip, Rental, Risk), avoiding unstructured text blobs.

Any time text overlays a property image (like Price or ROI on home cards), a vertical expo-linear-gradient scrim MUST be applied behind the text (transparent to >=70% surfaceInverse) to ensure WCAG AAA readability.
Include subtle haptic feedback (impactLight) when toggling any of the investor filter chips.
